content: Just as human language learning uses explicit reflection on writing output to reinforce correct grammatical and narrative habits, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured, graded reflection framework from high school language arts writing workshops to improve self-correction quality. The orthographic spelling of 'reflexion' (the variant of 'reflection') itself can act as a tiny 'mirror' that lets LLMs detect inconsistent reasoning outputs by anchoring reflection to a explicit, distinct marker of self-examination.
